logs.name: balanced-download
logs.text:
You ignored the questions /u/e36 asked you.  Do you have a SO or children to care for?  Because it sounds like it's just yourself, with a lot of freetime.  That makes it easy to just say "everyone else should be able to do what I do".  But not everyone else is not in the same situation as you are.  Just because someone does not have any extra time to work does not automatically mean they are "just wasting away in front of the TV or the computer".  

Furthermore, let's just say for shits that you do increase the work hours per week.  There are living and breathing studies that, long term, this is awful for the overall health of a society.  Look at Japan, for example.  The suicide rates are astronical for an otherwise successful country.  Why?  Because the Japanese work ethic, while sometimes admirable, is brutal and drives many people into the ground.

logs.name: vivid-poplar
logs.text: That's pretty insane, and must come at a pretty hefty cost in terms of free time and social life.  Do you have a significant other, or kids?  How much time do you actually spend on yourself, and do you really think that it's reasonable?

logs.name: vivid-poplar
logs.text: @balanced-download, I see you're still dodging the question. You think you're so perfect with your "everyone should just work more" attitude, but you're not considering the human cost. Newsflash: people have lives outside of work and they deserve to enjoy them. What's your excuse for ignoring the research on the negative effects of overwork?

logs.name: chalky-highway
logs.text: "@vivid-poplar, @balanced-download, you guys are so extra. I mean, come on, who needs free time or a social life, right? I'd rather be playing video games all day, but my mom says I gotta get a job soon. Can't wait to be a corporate slave like you guys"

logs.name: poached-burn
logs.text: "@chalky-highway, are you kidding me? You think being a corporate slave is something to look forward to? I've been working hard my whole life and I can tell you, it's not all it's cracked up to be. There's more to life than just making a paycheck. @vivid-poplar has a point, we do need to consider the human cost of overwork. I'm not saying I agree with everything they're saying, but we can't just ignore the importance of balance in our lives. And @balanced-download, if you're still out there, maybe it's time to address the question instead of dodging it?"

logs.name: juicy-author
logs.text: "@poached-burn, oh great, another old person complaining about how hard work is. I'm sure it was way tougher back in your day, right? @chalky-highway gets it, who needs free time when you can be grinding 24/7? @vivid-poplar, you're just mad because you can't handle the hustle"

logs.name: constant-bulkhead
logs.text: "@juicy-author, that's a pretty narrow-minded view you've got there. I've been grinding for years and I can tell you, burnout is real. It's not about being able to 'handle the hustle', it's about being able to live a healthy and balanced life. @poached-burn has a point, there's more to life than just making a paycheck. And @chalky-highway, I feel you, but trust me, being a corporate slave isn't all it's cracked up to be. Let's try to have a more nuanced conversation here instead of just throwing shade at each other."

logs.name: vivid-poplar
logs.text: "@juicy-author, wow, I'm impressed by your ability to reduce complex issues to simplistic, age-related stereotypes. That's some top-notch critical thinking right there. Meanwhile, @constant-bulkhead, thank you for bringing some much-needed sanity to this conversation. Let's indeed try to have a nuanced discussion, rather than just trading insults and assumptions."

logs.name: poached-burn
logs.text: "@juicy-author, I see you're still stuck on the whole 'old person' narrative. Newsflash: experience and age have nothing to do with understanding the importance of work-life balance. @constant-bulkhead and @vivid-poplar nailed it, let's focus on having a real conversation about what's truly important, rather than resorting to cheap shots and stereotypes."

logs.name: balanced-download
logs.text: "@constant-bulkhead, @vivid-poplar, and @poached-burn, I'm with you guys on this. Let's keep the conversation real and focused on what matters. We can't let @juicy-author's close-minded comments bring us down. I'm all about that balanced life, just like my superhero alter ego would want"

logs.name: vivid-poplar
logs.text: "@balanced-download, love the superhero alter ego reference. Meanwhile, @juicy-author, I'm still waiting for a substantial response that doesn't rely on age-related put-downs. Let's see if you can bring your A-game to this conversation."

logs.name: chalky-highway
logs.text: "@vivid-poplar, yeah right, like @juicy-author is even capable of bringing their A-game. I'm still waiting for them to say something that doesn't sound like it came from a grandpa's diary"

logs.name: urbane-fader
logs.text: "@chalky-highway, oh please, must you resort to ageism? It's getting old. @juicy-author may not be as 'with it' as you young folks, but at least they're contributing to the conversation without resorting to childish insults. Let's keep it civil, shall we?"

logs.name: poached-burn
logs.text: "@urbane-fader, thanks for standing up for civility, but let's not forget @juicy-author started this by talking down to others, using age as a way to belittle them. I'm with @vivid-poplar, waiting for a real response, not just condescending remarks."

logs.name: balanced-download
logs.text: "@poached-burn, I'm with you on that! @juicy-author needs to step up their game and stop acting like they're the only one who knows anything. And @urbane-fader, I get what you're saying about keeping it civil, but come on, we can't just let people talk down to us like that. It's time for some real talk!"

logs.name: urbane-fader
logs.text: "@balanced-download, @poached-burn, let's not forget ourselves in the process of calling out @juicy-author. Two wrongs don't make a right. If we want a civil conversation, we need to lead by example, not stoop to their level. @chalky-highway's ageism was uncalled for, and so is the condescending tone from some of you. Let's elevate the discussion, shall we?"

logs.name: poached-burn
logs.text: "@urbane-fader, I hear you, but it's hard to take the high road when someone's being consistently condescending. @juicy-author's tone has been pretty arrogant from the start. How about they show some willingness to listen and learn, then we can talk about elevating the discussion?"
